title: Helianthemum cinereum
intro: Helianthemum cinereum, also known as rockrose, is a low-growing, perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum cinereum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with rockrose include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Helianthemum cinereum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a part of the genus Helianthemum, which comprises around 100 species of rockrose plants. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Helianthemum cinereum is a low-growing, shrubby perennial with small, fragrant flowers that are typically pink or white in color. As a member of the Cistaceae family, Helianthemum cinereum is closely related to other rockrose species, such as Cistus and Halimium.
uses: Helianthemum cinereum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. Its leaves and stems contain compounds with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, the plant has been employed to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. However, further research is necessary to fully validate its efficacy and safety. More studies are needed to confirm the medicinal properties and potential applications of Helianthemum cinereum.
